name: Brother INKvestment Tank MFC-J4335DW
bestFor: Home office users who want cartridge-based simplicity without a mandatory ink plan.
linuxNotes: Brother publishes Linux drivers for many models; confirm exact model support before purchase.
riskNotes: Cartridges are still proprietary, but the ownership model is clearer than subscription-first printers.

name: Epson EcoTank ET-2850
bestFor: Households that print often and prefer refillable tanks over cartridges.
linuxNotes: Epson has Linux driver coverage for many EcoTank models; verify distribution support.
riskNotes: Lower ink cost, but maintenance and printhead care matter if the printer sits unused.

name: Canon MegaTank G3270
bestFor: Budget buyers who want refillable ink tanks and basic home printing.
linuxNotes: Linux support varies by region and driver package; check before buying.
riskNotes: Good ink economics, but software and driver support can be less predictable.

name: Brother HL-L2460DW
bestFor: Text-heavy printing with low fuss and no color ink maintenance.
linuxNotes: Brother laser printers are commonly used with Linux, CUPS, AirPrint, and driverless setups.
riskNotes: Black-and-white only; check toner chip behavior and third-party toner compatibility.
